summary: Both REQ-47 and REQ-48 appear to be implemented. REQ-47 is addressed via a new generateStarterEquipment() function in lootGenerator.ts and integration in CharacterCreation.tsx. REQ-48 is addressed by replacing the drop rate slider with a static informational display in DungeonSelectScreen.tsx. However, there are notable gaps: REQ-47 requires items to be stored in equipped slots on the backend character record, but the diff shows only frontend localStorage storage with no backend equipped-slots structure. Additionally, DungeonSelectScreen.tsx still contains slider-related handler code, raising questions about whether the slider was fully removed from the rendered UI.

missing_requirements.requirement: The backend character record must store the starter equipment in the same equipped slots structure used by all other equipment.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows starter equipment is saved only to localStorage via saveStarterEquipmentForCharacter in CharacterCreation.tsx. There is no evidence of backend (main.mo) changes to store equipped slots or starter equipment in the on-chain character record. The build request constraint explicitly states the backend character record must store the starter equipment in the same equipped slots structure used by all other equipment.

missing_requirements.requirement: Starter armor must provide enough base defense to meaningfully reduce incoming damage from level 1 monsters which currently deal approximately 13 damage.
missing_requirements.reason: The lootGenerator.ts summary states the Worn Chestplate has 5 base defense. Whether 5 base defense meaningfully reduces approximately 13 incoming damage depends on the combat formula, and 5 defense against 13 damage may not constitute noticeably less than 13 damage depending on the implementation.

hallucinated_features.description: DungeonSelectScreen.tsx retains a handleSliderChange handler function and associated monster level slider state management despite REQ-48 requiring complete removal of the drop rate slider.
hallucinated_features.reason: The diff content for DungeonSelectScreen.tsx shows a handleSliderChange function still present in the code, suggesting slider-related logic was not fully removed even if the rendered slider UI element was replaced with static text.
